creator: samm@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 09/05/2010 08:12 PM, Garrett Wollman wrote:
> <<On Sun, 05 Sep 2010 10:47:40 +0200, Alex Samorukov<samm@os2.kiev.ua>  said:
>
>    
>> 1) Source tree needs to be exist to build smartmontools with cissio
>> support. It will build w/o source tree, but cissio will be disabled by
>> configure script.
>>      
> That's a bug.  NOTHING in userland should compile against the source
> tree.  If you need a header file that's not installed, work with a src
> developer to get that header installed.
>    
Very funny.  I don`t think so. Try to argue your position next time, 
this may help.  BTW - this "bug" was in smartmontools from early 
beginning and only you hit this condition - i think that is very good 
indicator of its importance, isn`t it?

Your commit will break smartmontools functionality, as i mentioned 
before. Thats why i`m against it. Try to read before posting.

The required file was never included in the /usr/include. And it was 
changed more then once from the beginning.
>    
>> 2) Out of sync sources is not a problem of smartmontools, but user.
>>      
> No, it's a bug.  *Nothing else cares*, nor is it legitimate to care.
> /usr/src often does not match the running kernel, particularly in the
> middle of an upgrade cycle.
>    
No its not a bug. *Nothing else cares*. Do you like that discussion?

You can troll this way as long as you want. And of course, you VERY need 
to upgrade smartmontools in the middle of the system upgrade cycle.
> That's not a reasonable answer.
>    
Press up button.
> -GAWollman
>
>
>
